meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Testing Knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to overcome a lack of testing knowledge. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the fear of testing begins by automating the user onboarding flow. By treating onboarding as a series of verifiable states, you build a foundation for automated testing. Use Antigravity to define these states and Claude Code to write tests that ensure your users reach the 'Aha!' moment every time.
content.intro:
You’ve shipped your first feature, but the silence is deafening. You aren't worried about scaling; you're paralyzed by the 'no testing' void. Every time you push code, you hold your breath, wondering if your onboarding flow—the very heart of your app—has silently shattered for new users.

This isn't just about bugs; it's about confidence. We are going to transform your onboarding flow from a fragile, manual guesswork process into a robust, test-driven system. By leveraging AI-assisted tools, you will build a safety net that protects your users while you learn the architecture of professional-grade software development.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user's path. We define onboarding as a series of discrete states (e.g., 'signed_up', 'profile_completed', 'first_action_taken').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state machine definition for a SaaS user onboarding flow involving three distinct steps. Map out the transitions and necessary data requirements for each state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a TypeScript state machine for onboarding steps.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Onboarding UI
steps.description: Build the UI components that correspond to your states, ensuring each component is isolated and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular React component structure for a multi-step onboarding wizard that consumes the state machine defined in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add ./src/components/onboarding --prompt 'Create modular components for the onboarding wizard.'

steps.title: Instrument State Transitions
steps.description: Add logic to trigger state changes. This is where we catch errors early by validating user input at every transition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the transition logic for the onboarding wizard. Include robust error handling for failed state transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts --prompt 'Implement state transition logic with error handling.'

steps.title: Automate Onboarding Tests
steps.description: Since we lack traditional testing experience, we use AI to generate end-to-end (E2E) tests that simulate a user completing the flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Playwright tests that simulate a user moving through the entire onboarding flow, validating the state at each step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --prompt 'Create E2E tests for the onboarding flow using Playwright.'

steps.title: Continuous Validation Loop
steps.description: Set up a pipeline where every pull request triggers your onboarding tests, ensuring your flow never breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD workflow for GitHub Actions that runs the Playwright tests created in the previous step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create .github/workflows/onboarding-test.yml --prompt 'Setup CI for onboarding tests.'

pro_tips: Always test the 'happy path' first, but treat the 'failed state' as your primary architectural concern.
Use AI to generate mock data for your tests; it saves hours of manual entry and edge-case discovery.
If a test fails, don't just fix the code; use Claude Code to identify the architectural drift that caused it.
Keep your onboarding components small—if a component is over 100 lines, it's too complex to test effectively.
Treat your test suite as a living document; update it every time you add a new step to the onboarding sequence.
content.next_steps: Integrate analytics tracking into your onboarding flow to see where users drop off, then use your new testing suite to iterate on those specific steps.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to ship with confidence and the framework to master automated testing as you scale. Don't let the fear of breaking things stop your progress—start automating your success today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Build the UI components that correspond to your states, ensuring each component is isolated and testable.
